title: Mespilus heldreichii
intro: Mespilus heldreichii, also known as the Dalmatian whitebeam, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Balkan Peninsula and parts of Eastern Europe. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Mespilus heldreichii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include infusions, teas, and extracts, which are used to support digestive health and relieve various symptoms associated with gastrointestinal disorders.
